 {"id":"bd-69cuc","title":"Task: Add TOON tool adapter (detect/version)","description":"## Overview
If TOON CLI is used, add a tools adapter for detecting `toon` binary and version.

## Details
- CLI is provided by `@toon-format/cli` (binary: `toon`).
- Prefer `toon --version` for version parsing; fallback to `toon --help` if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects installed `toon` binary
- Parses version output
- Used by `--robot-format=auto` to decide availability","notes":"UPDATE per bd-2csfi decision: This task is no longer needed.

Decision was made to use Go library (toon-format/toon-go) instead of CLI wrapper. Since there's no CLI binary to detect, this tool adapter task is unnecessary.

Recommend closing as 'not-needed' or 'superseded'.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:35.705545571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T01:47:06.680028748Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T01:47:06.679986348Z","close_reason":"Obsolete: TOON encoding is in-process Go; no external toon CLI to detect.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:35.734696036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-69cuc","depends_on_id":"bd-2csfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:16:50.832289159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6fek","title":"Error Handling Tests: ntm init recovery scenarios","description":"## Error Handling and Recovery Tests for ntm init

### Error Scenarios to Test
1. **Filesystem errors**
   - Permission denied on directory creation
   - Disk full during config write
   - Read-only filesystem
   - Network filesystem latency

2. **Config validation errors**
   - Invalid TOML syntax recovery
   - Missing required fields
   - Type mismatches in config
   - Circular template references

3. **Graceful degradation**
   - Continue with defaults if config unreadable
   - Warn but proceed if optional dir creation fails
   - Fallback if flywheel tool unavailable

### Recovery Verification
- Test that partial init can be resumed
- Test that corrupted config is detected and reported
- Test cleanup on failure (no orphaned files)

### Logging Requirements
log.Printf("[ERROR] %s: %v", operation, err)
log.Printf("[RECOVER] Attempting: %s", recoveryAction)
log.Printf("[CLEANUP] Removing: %s", path)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:22.883192270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T06:19:13.519261661Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:22.903861158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-ubhl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:19:13.519233508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qk8","title":"Task: Implement ntm level command","description":"## Overview
Create the 'ntm level' command for viewing and changing proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/level.go:
   - ntm level - show current tier and progress
   - ntm level up - promote to next tier
   - ntm level down - demote to previous tier
   - ntm level set <tier> - set specific tier (1, 2, 3 or apprentice, journeyman, master)

2. Output format for 'ntm level':
   Current proficiency: Journeyman (Tier 2)
   
   Usage stats:
     Commands run: 150
     Sessions created: 25
     Prompts sent: 200
     Using NTM since: 15 days
   
   Next tier: Master
     Unlocks: Robot mode, internal debugging, experimental features
     Suggested milestone: Run 50 more commands or 30 days of use
   
   Run 'ntm level master' to unlock all features now.

3. Promotion flow:
   - Confirm before tier change
   - Show what changes (new commands unlocked or hidden)
   - Log promotion to history

## Implementation Notes
- Use cobra subcommands for level operations
- Support both numeric (1,2,3) and named (apprentice, journeyman, master) tiers
- Add emoji support when terminal supports it

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm level shows current status
- ntm level up/down work correctly
- Promotion history is recorded
- Unit tests for all subcommands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:53.536644317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075203473Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:11:16.075154591Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm level command in internal/cli/level.go with show/up/down/set subcommands, added to root.go and tiers registry","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-1s9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:30.003353366Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6qk8","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:53.565777119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6s038","title":"TOON: Update documentation and examples","description":"Update README and other documentation for TOON support.

## Documentation Updates

### README.md
- Add TOON output format to robot mode section
- Include examples with both JSON and TOON output
- Document performance benefits

### Example Updates
```bash
# JSON output (default)
ntm --robot-list-panes
{"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}

# TOON output (40-60% fewer tokens)
ntm --robot-list-panes --robot-output-format toon
panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]
```

### Configuration Documentation
Document [robot.output] section with all options.

### Migration Guide
For users who have scripts parsing JSON output:
- JSON remains default
- Explicit --robot-output-format json for guaranteed JSON
- Recommendation: update scripts to support TOON for cost savings

 {"id":"bd-6wc0a","title":"TOON Integration: Token-Optimized Object Notation for Robot Mode","description":"Integrate TOON (toon_rust) to reduce token usage in robot mode output by 40-60%.

## Project Location
/dp/toon_rust (Rust library + CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Robot mode outputs JSON for every command (~60 commands). This JSON is:
- Read by AI agents (tokens cost money)
- Transmitted over network (bandwidth)
- Stored in logs (storage)

JSON is verbose with repeated keys, quotes, whitespace.

## What TOON Does

TOON is a token-efficient serialization format:
- 40-60% fewer tokens than equivalent JSON
- Designed specifically for LLM consumption
- Preserves type information
- Human-readable (unlike binary formats)
- Round-trip compatible with JSON

### Example
JSON:
```json
{"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}
```

TOON:
```
panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: Output Format Flag (Recommended)
Add --robot-output-format flag:
- json (default, backwards compatible)
- toon (new, token-optimized)

```bash
ntm --robot-list-panes --robot-output-format toon
```

### Option B: Config-Based Default
Set default output format in config:
```toml
[robot]
output_format = "toon"
```

### Option C: Auto-Detect
If agent supports TOON, use TOON automatically.

## Implementation Plan
1. Add toon_rust as Go dependency (via CGO/FFI or subprocess)
2. Create serialization wrapper that supports both JSON and TOON
3. Add --robot-output-format flag to all robot commands
4. Add config option for default format
5. Update documentation

## Considerations
- Backwards compatibility: existing scripts expect JSON
- Parsing: agents need TOON parser (or we convert back to JSON for them)
- Errors: TOON errors should fall back to JSON
- caut integration: if caut parses robot output, it needs TOON support","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:51.295366550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:51.325372571Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wc0a","depends_on_id":"bd-1k3ga","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:51.325340690Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-71cpj","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview
Generate shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, and fish shells.

## User Story
As a user who frequently uses ntm, I want tab completion so that I can:
- Quickly complete command names (ntm sp[TAB] -> ntm spawn)
- Complete flag names (ntm spawn --c[TAB] -> --cc)
- Complete session names from running sessions
- Complete agent names/IDs for targeted operations

## Implementation
Using cobra's built-in completion generation:
- Add completion subcommand supporting bash, zsh, fish, powershell
- Register custom completion functions for dynamic values (sessions, agents)

## Dynamic Completions
- Session names: List active tmux sessions
- Agent IDs: List agents in current/specified session
- Bead IDs: List open beads for assign command
- Agent types: cc, cod, gmi

## CLI Usage
```bash
# Generate bash completion
ntm completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/ntm

# Generate zsh completion
ntm completion zsh > /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_ntm

# Generate fish completion
ntm completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/ntm.fish
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ntm completion bash outputs valid bash completion script
- [ ] ntm completion zsh outputs valid zsh completion script
- [ ] ntm completion fish outputs valid fish completion script
- [ ] Session names complete dynamically
- [ ] Agent IDs complete dynamically
- [ ] Flag names and values complete correctly
- [ ] Documentation in help output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:01:08.374795401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:01:08.391648853Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-71cpj","depends_on_id":"bd-22nh8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:01:08.391615961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-77bxi","title":"TOON: Research Go integration approach (CGO vs subprocess)","description":"Evaluate approaches for calling toon_rust from Go code.

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

## Recommendation

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

## Research Tasks
1. Verify toon CLI exists and has encode/decode commands
2. Benchmark subprocess overhead
3. Evaluate if CGO is needed for any use case
4. Check if toon_rust has WASM build

## Deliverable
Decision document with benchmarks and recommendation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:02:39.102158806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:02:39.180046843Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-77bxi","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:02:39.178660552Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
